name: Sichuan Peanut Sauce Noodles
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Lime juice, or red wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1 teaspoon of hot chili oil in a small bowl.
2. Add 1/2 cup water and stir to combine.
3. In a food processor, coarsely chop the peanuts until you have about 1/4 cup remaining.
4. Add the chopped peanuts back into the food processor with the ginger, garlic, salt, sugar, sesame oil, peanut oil, dark soy sauce, lime juice, and chili oil.
5. Process until smooth and creamy, adding more water if needed to reach desired consistency.
6. In a large pot, bring 4 quarts of water to a boil. Add the egg noodles and stir gently to separate them. Cook for 1 minute, then drain thoroughly under cold running water.
7. Rinse the drained noodles and set aside.
8. To serve, toss the noodles with the peanut sauce. Divide among plates and garnish with sliced green onions, shredded poached chicken, julienned blanched green beans, and chopped coriander leaves.
9. Serve at room temperature.
Serves 6 as a side dish.
